avalancheActivity.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase during the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften during the day. In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface.
As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be a gradual increase in the avalanche danger. Wet snow slides and avalanches are possible, even medium-sized ones.
This applies in particular on very steep north facing slopes below approximately 2600 m, and on very steep east, south and west facing slopes at elevated altitudes. In high Alpine regions there will be an increase in the danger of moist and wet avalanches within the current danger level.
Backcountry tours and ascents to alpine cabins should be started early and concluded timely.

snowpackStructure.comment:
As a consequence of highly fluctuating temperatures the snowpack consolidated. This applies in particular on steep sunny slopes at high altitude, and in all aspects at intermediate altitudes. The snowpack is fairly homogeneous and its surface has a strong crust. Hardly any more avalanches were reported.
This situation gave rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2700 m.
The amount of snow is subject to significant local variations.
Especially on sunny slopes below the tree line no snow is lying. On shady slopes below the tree line hardly any snow is lying.
Especially on the colder slopes above the treeline, in the valley basins and headlands that have been less affected by stormy winds in recent weeks, there is excellent snow cover.
Weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack in particular at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions.

tendency.highlights: Monday: Until late morning the weather will be partly cloudy. Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be reduced.

regions.name: B - VALLI DI GRESSONEY, AYAS, CHAMPORCHER

avalancheActivity.highlights: Increase in danger of wet avalanches in the course of the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be an increase in the danger of wet avalanches. In particular at the base of rock walls avalanches can be released naturally.
Individual avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2500 m. Such avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise.
The danger of ground avalanches will increase during the day.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust only at high altitudes and will soften during the day. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise to increasing and thorough wetting of the snowpack below approximately 3000 m.
Steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2500 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack.
Below approximately 2000 m from a snow sport perspective, in most cases insufficient snow is lying.

tendency.highlights: Sunday: The avalanche danger will persist.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The weather will be mild. The spring-like weather conditions will give rise to softening of the snowpack.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls, and in gullies and bowls. Individual wet and gliding avalanches are possible. Places where weaknesses exist in the old snowpack are unfavourable.
Backcountry tours should be concluded early.

snowpackStructure.comment: Snow cover is absent or patchy in many areas, especially on southern slopes. Due to the temperatures, the snowpack is steadily decreasing. The weather conditions will give rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack. Individual weak layers exist in the snowpack on shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: Continuous warming. The weather will be partly cloudy.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust only at high altitudes and will soften during the day. Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack on steep shady slopes.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on steep shady slopes above approximately 2800 m.
As a consequence of warming during the day and the solar radiation, the likelihood of wet avalanches being released will increase in particular on steep shady slopes below approximately 2800 m.
Medium-sized avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The surface of the snowpack will soften during the day.
Steep shady slopes above approximately 2800 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack.
Below approximately 2100 m from a snow sport perspective, in most cases insufficient snow is lying.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet and gliding snow require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment: In particular in east to north to west facing aspects more medium-sized gliding avalanches and wet snow slides are possible especially in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place.

snowpackStructure.comment: Below approximately 1800 m only a little snow is lying. In gullies and bowls at high altitude there is still a very large amount of snow. The spring-like weather conditions gave rise to significant settling of the snowpack. Backcountry tours should be started and concluded early.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Increase in danger of wet avalanches in the course of the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In the late morning a favourable avalanche situation will be encountered over a wide area.

As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be only a slight increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Wet avalanches can in isolated cases be released by a single winter sport participant. This applies on very steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes below approximately 2500 m, as well as on very steep west and east facing slopes below approximately 2800 m, but in isolated cases also on very steep sunny slopes at elevated altitudes.

Avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2500 m. These places are very rare but are difficult to recognise.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be quite good. The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften during the day. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise to gradual moistening of the snowpack at intermediate and high altitudes.

Shady slopes above approximately 2500 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack at elevated altitudes.

On steep sunny slopes as well as below approximately 1800 m hardly any snow is lying.

tendency.highlights: Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be quite good. The danger of wet avalanches will increase a little during the day.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Increase in danger of wet avalanches in the late morning.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be an increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Wet avalanches can in isolated cases be released by a single winter sport participant. This applies on very steep shady slopes below approximately 3000 m, as well as on very steep sunny slopes at elevated altitudes.

Individual avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2500 m. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable. The avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be quite good. The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften during the day. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise to gradual moistening of the snowpack at intermediate and high altitudes.

Shady slopes above approximately 2500 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack at elevated altitudes.

In particular in the Prealps as well as below approximately 1800 m hardly any snow is lying.

tendency.highlights: Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be reduced. The danger of wet avalanches will already be elevated in the early morning.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Increase in danger of wet avalanches in the course of the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In the late morning a favourable avalanche situation will prevail.
As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be only a slight increase in the danger of wet avalanches. This applies on very steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes below approximately 2500 m, as well as on very steep west and east facing slopes below approximately 2800 m, but in isolated cases also on very steep sunny slopes at elevated altitudes.
Wet avalanches can in isolated cases be released by a single winter sport participant. In very isolated cases these can release the weakly bonded old snow as well and reach large size. This applies on very steep shady slopes below approximately 2500 m.

Dry avalanches can additionally in very isolated cases be released, mostly by large additional loads, especially on very steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes above approximately 2500 m. The avalanche prone locations are very rare but are difficult to recognise.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be quite good. The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften during the day. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise to gradual moistening of the snowpack at intermediate and high altitudes.

Shady slopes above approximately 2500 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack.

At low and intermediate altitudes hardly any snow is lying.

tendency.highlights: Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be quite good. The danger of wet avalanches will increase a little during the day.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase during the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and will soften earlier than the day before.
As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be a gradual increase in the avalanche danger. Wet snow slides and avalanches are possible, even medium-sized ones. This applies in particular on very steep north facing slopes below approximately 2500 m, and on very steep east, south and west facing slopes below approximately 2900 m.
In high Alpine regions there will be an increase in the danger of moist and wet avalanches within the current danger level.
Backcountry tours and ascents to alpine cabins should be started early and concluded timely.
In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface.

snowpackStructure.comment:
As a consequence of highly fluctuating temperatures the snowpack consolidated. This applies in particular on steep sunny slopes at high altitude, and in all aspects at intermediate altitudes. The snowpack is fairly homogeneous and its surface has a melt-freeze crust. This applies in particular in the early morning.
Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to increasing moistening of the snowpack.
Below approximately 2200 m only a little snow is lying on south facing slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust only at high altitudes and will already soften in the late morning.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The surface of the snowpack will only just freeze and will soften during the day.
In isolated cases the avalanches are small.

snowpackStructure.comment: As a consequence of mild temperatures the snowpack consolidated. As a consequence of warming during the day there will be an increase in the danger of moist and wet avalanches.

avalancheActivity.comment:
Only isolated wet avalanches are possible. Caution is to be exercised on extremely steep shady slopes. Mostly avalanches are rather small.

Below approximately 2000 m from a snow sport perspective, in most cases insufficient snow is lying.

snowpackStructure.comment: The surface of the snowpack will soften during the day.

tendency.highlights: Sunday: The avalanche danger will persist.

avalancheActivity.comment: Only isolated wet avalanches are possible. Caution is to be exercised on extremely steep shady slopes. Mostly avalanches are rather small.

snowpackStructure.comment: The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften during the day.

tendency.highlights: Low avalanche danger will prevail. Wet snow requires caution.
